====================================================== START - 1 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

csrw 0x747, x10
lpad x11, x10, x12

bootloader/code.S: Assembler messages:
bootloader/code.S:38: Error: unrecognized opcode `lpad x11,x10,x12', extension `zicfilp' required
make: *** [<builtin>: bootloader/code.o] Error 1

Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
csrw 0x747, x10
or x11, x10, x12
======================================================= END - 1 ========================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 2 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li x10, -1
csrw 0x747, x10
csrr x11, 0x747
li x12, 0xfffffffffcfffffcf8
and x12, x11, x12

bootloader/code.S: Assembler messages:
bootloader/code.S:40: Error: unsupported large constant
make: *** [<builtin>: bootloader/code.o] Error 1

Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li x10, -1
csrw 0x747, x10
csrr x11, 0x747
lui x12, 0xFCFFF
addi x12, x12, 0xF8
and x12, x11, x12
======================================================= END - 2 ========================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 3 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 1
csrw 0x747, t0
jal ra, 0x100000

riscv64-unknown-elf-ld: warning: bootloader/bootloader has a LOAD segment with RWX permissions
bootloader/code.o: in function `test_start':
(.text+0x48): relocation truncated to fit: R_RISCV_JAL against `*UND*'
make: *** [Makefile:52: bootloader/bootloader] Error 1

Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 1
csrw 0x747, t0
auipc t1, %pcrel_hi(0x100000)
addi t1, t1, %pcrel_lo(0x100000)
jalr ra, t1, 0
======================================================= END - 3 ========================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 4 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

csrw stvec, 0x80001003
csrr x10, stvec

bootloader/code.S: Assembler messages:
bootloader/code.S:37: Error: improper CSRxI immediate (2147487747)
make: *** [<builtin>: bootloader/code.o] Error 1

Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li x5, 0x80001003
csrw stvec, x5
csrr x10, stvec
======================================================= END - 4 ========================================================

====================================================== START - 5 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

call machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, mcause



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
call machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause
======================================================= END - 5 ========================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 6 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 1
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, (1 << 1)
csrs 0x100, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 1
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 6 ========================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 7 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 1
csrw 0x747, t0
auipc t1, %pcrel_hi(0x100000)
addi t1, t1, %pcrel_lo(0x100000)
jalr ra, t1, 0

/tmp/cckDme2g.s: Assembler messages:
/tmp/cckDme2g.s: Internal error (Segmentation fault).
Please report this bug.
make: *** [<builtin>: bootloader/code.o] Error 1

Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 1
csrw 0x747, t0
li t1, 0x100000
jalr ra, t1, 0

====================================================== START - 8 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

call machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:

# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========

li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause

====================================================== START - 9 =======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 1
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 1
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, 32          # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 9 ========================================================

====================================================== START - 11 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 1
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, 32          # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, 32          # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 11 =======================================================

csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!

li t1, 32          # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi

wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

csrs 0x300, t2

addi x0, x0, 0

addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi

====================================================== START - 16 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
machine_to_supervisor:
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause
======================================================= END - 16 =======================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 17 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x105, t0
li t1, 32          # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2           # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 17 =======================================================
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 18 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
machine_to_supervisor:
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw stvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
machine_to_supervisor:
    li t0, 0x80001001
    csrw mtvec, t0
    li t1, 0x20
    csrs sip, t1
    li t2, 0x2
    csrs sstatus, t2
    wfi
    csrr x10, scause
======================================================= END - 18 =======================================================

====================================================== START - 19 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 19 =======================================================

########################################################################################################################
########################################################################################################################
====================================================== START - 20 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
machine_to_supervisor:
li t0, 0x80001001
csrw mtvec, t0
li t1, 0x20
csrs sip, t1
li t2, 0x2
csrs sstatus, t2
wfi
csrr x10, scause



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
jal ra, machine_to_supervisor
machine_to_supervisor:
    li t0, 0x80001001
    csrw mtvec, t0
    li t1, 0x20
    csrs sip, t1
    li t2, 0x2
    csrs sstatus, t2
    wfi
    csrr x10, scause
    j .
======================================================= END - 20 =======================================================

====================================================== START - 21 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi

====================================================== START - 23 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # changed to a valid writable CSR (mtvec)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x104, t1
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # mtvec (writable CSR)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x304, t1          # mie (valid writable CSR)
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 23 =======================================================

====================================================== START - 25 ======================================================
=======
PROMPT:
=======
============================================================
For RISC-V, minimally fix the test case below while PRESERVING the ORIGINAL INSTRUCTION and OPERANDS whenever possible:

li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # mtvec (writable CSR)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x304, t1          # mie (valid writable CSR)
li t2, 2                # (1 << 1)
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi



Do not output any introduction. Follow the format above.

Notes:
+ Remember, output must be ASCII-only text.
+ DO NOT USE .globl, _start, labels, directives, or PC-relative expressions; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use ```assembly or ```asm just write the assembly code
+ Write the full assembly code, DO NOT write something like this:
/* ... other code ... */
# ... other existing code ...
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
li t0, 0x80000100
ori t0, t0, 0
csrw 0x305, t0          # mtvec (writable CSR)
li t1, 32               # (1 << 5)
csrs 0x304, t1          # mie (valid writable CSR)
li t2, 8                # (1 << 3) set MIE in mstatus
csrs 0x300, t2
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
addi x0, x0, 0
auipc x10, 0
wfi
======================================================= END - 25 =======================================================
